name: @prune/context-utility
description: F1 — Context-Utility Model (CUM). A standing, outcome-learned per-atom utility store: tracks each context atom's realized contribution to ACCEPTED output as a decayed Beta-Binomial empirical-Bayes posterior, queried by every selector for a prior. The learning is caller-fed (the host supplies the accept/reject/cite-back verdict); the estimation and serving are pure deterministic counting — no model call, no regex. Cold-start and unknown atoms return null (selectors run unchanged); advisory-only, floor-safe.
